name: Montamarta from Zamora – circular via the Ricobayo Reservoir
name_original: Montamarta desde Zamora – circular por el Embalse de Ricobayo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Campos-Pan explores a landscape shaped by the Douro river and its tributaries, featuring diverse terrain from urban routes to natural reserves. The region includes areas around Zamora, characterized by riverine paths, reservoirs, and agricultural lands. Cyclists can find routes traversing open countryside, forests, and wetlands. The elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes offering more significant climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Campos-Pan The most popular touring cycling route is City tour by Zamora - circular walk by river Douro, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 54 minutes to complete. This route offers an easy ride along the river, showcasing urban and natural scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Montamarta from Zamora – circular via the Ricobayo Reservoir, a difficult 48.5 miles (78.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and expansive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Natural Reserve of the Lagunas de Villafáfila – circular from the Laguna Grande, a 30.3 miles (48.8 km) trail leading through wetlands and open plains, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Campos-Pan is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and natural reserves, offering a mix of flat sections and rolling hills. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy city loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 450 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campos-Pan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Campos-Pan region?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes in the Campos-Pan region, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes explore a landscape shaped by the Douro river and its tributaries, featuring varied terrain from urban paths to natural reserves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Campos-Pan?
A: Touring cycling around Campos-Pan is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and natural reserves. You'll find a mix of flat sections along the river, rolling hills, and some routes with more significant elevation changes. The region includes areas around Zamora, characterized by riverine paths, reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and agricultural lands, as well as wetlands in areas like the Natural Reserve of the Lagunas de Villafáfila.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, Campos-Pan offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. There are 9 easy routes available, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037042545/city-tour-by-zamora-circular-walk-by-river-douro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour by Zamora - circular walk by river Douro&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.3-mile (15.0 km) trail offering a pleasant ride along the river.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Campos-Pan?
A: The touring cycling routes in Campos-Pan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from easy river paths to more challenging routes with expansive views, and the opportunity to explore both urban and natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Campos-Pan are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037042545/city-tour-by-zamora-circular-walk-by-river-douro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour by Zamora - circular walk by river Douro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036943430/natural-reserve-of-the-lagunas-de-villafafila-circular-from-the-laguna-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Lagunas de Villafáfila – circular from the Laguna Grande&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes in Campos-Pan, you can discover several natural attractions. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036943430/natural-reserve-of-the-lagunas-de-villafafila-circular-from-the-laguna-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Lagunas de Villafáfila&lt;/a&gt; offers unique wetland landscapes.

Q: What cultural or historical landmarks can I explore while cycling in Campos-Pan?
A: The region offers several cultural and historical landmarks. In Zamora, you can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Zamora&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Cathedral (Catedral de San Salvador)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037042545/city-tour-by-zamora-circular-walk-by-river-douro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour by Zamora - circular walk by river Douro&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to see some of these urban sights.

Q: Are there any routes with significant elevation gain for more challenging rides?
A: Yes, for cyclists seeking a challenge, there are routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037047890/montamarta-from-zamora-circular-via-the-ricobayo-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta from Zamora – circular via the Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.5-mile (78.1 km) path featuring expansive views and considerable climbing, making it a favorite among experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I find good viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Campos-Pan?
A: Absolutely. Routes that traverse higher ground or pass by reservoirs often offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037047890/montamarta-from-zamora-circular-via-the-ricobayo-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta from Zamora – circular via the Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views of the reservoir and surrounding landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otero de Sariegos Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable viewpoint, especially for wildlife enthusiasts.

Q: Are there opportunities to observe wildlife while cycling in Campos-Pan?
A: Yes, particularly in the natural reserves and wetland areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036943430/natural-reserve-of-the-lagunas-de-villafafila-circular-from-the-laguna-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve of the Lagunas de Villafáfila&lt;/a&gt; is known for its birdlife and unique flora, offering excellent opportunities for wildlife observation while cycling through its open plains and wetlands.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in Campos-Pan?
A: One of the longer touring cycling routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037047890/montamarta-from-zamora-circular-via-the-ricobayo-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta from Zamora – circular via the Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 48.5 miles (78.1 km). This route offers a substantial ride for those looking for a longer adventure.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Valorio Forest?
A: Yes, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valorio Forest&lt;/a&gt; on a touring bicycle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivares Watermills – Valorio Forest loop from Zamora&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.1-mile (32.3 km) route that takes you through this natural area, offering a pleasant escape into nature near Zamora.
tips: Bridge over the Esla river. Inaugurated in 2007, the new Manzanal del Barco bridge left in ruins the old and narrow bridge built during the construction of the Ricobayo Reservoir over the Esla River in 1931 until its final closure to traffic in 2017.
The hermitage can be accessed by a cement path with a steep slope. Once at the top, there are some benches around the hermitage from where you can enjoy the views of the reservoir.
The stone bridge of Zamora crosses the Duero River through one of the widest areas, measuring 180 meters in length. It is made up of 15 pointed stone arches.
A modern bridge over the Douro River with spectacular views of the city above and the river landscape.
